content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

December 8, 1976

Mr. Melvin Shapiro
3701 Seventh Avenue, #6
San Diego, California 92103

Dear Mr. Shapiro:

Your letter of November 12, 1976, has been assigned to me for reply. Please excuse the delay of this response.

You ask whether members of the San Diego City Council were required to file Statements of Economic Interests in 1976. Government Code §87203 requires councilmembers to file an anniversary Statement of Economic Interests, disclosing investments, interests in real property, and income held or received in the previous 12 month period since the filing of the previous statement.

In checking our files, I find that the San Diego City Council has fully complied with the disclosure requirements under the Act. Copies of their anniversary Statements should be on file with the office of the City Clerk, and available for viewing by any member of the public.

Your second question was whether Planning Commissioners are "designated employees" required to file disclosure statements under a Conflict of Interest Code. Because of the nature of their duties, Planning Commissioners do make and participate in making governmental decisions, and as such must be designated for inclusion within a Conflict of Interest Code.

The deadlines for submission of Conflict of Interest Codes by local agencies are to be determined by local code reviewing bodies. The Act does not set any specific dates, but rather speaks in terms of "reasonable" deadline dates. Therefore, so long as the local code reviewing body does not abuse its discretionary power, it can set whatever date is reasonably convenient for compliance with the Act.

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441


Melvin Shapiro
December 8, 1976
                                                              Page Two


     Your last question concerns an opinion request made by
Councilman William P. Quigley of Merced.  In that request,
the Councilman asked the Commission if an attorney who is
a public official is required to disclose the identity of
a client pursuant to Government Code §87207(b), when,
although the client's name is a matter of public record,
the client requests that the attorney not disclose the
fact of his representation in the attorney's Statement
of Economic Interests.

     The Commission dismissed the opinion request based on
recently concluded litigation in the Mendocino County Superior
Court in the case of Hays v. Wood.  In Hays, the court con-
cluded that §87207(b)(2), as it applies to attorney-public
officials, is a valid provision of law, that it does not
violate the attorney-client privilege, the right of privacy
of either the attorney-official or his client, or the right
to equal protection of the law.  Although the case is now
on appeal, Mr. Quigley was asked to file an amended State-
ment of Economic Interests disclosing the information required
by this provision.  (Government Code §87207.)

     Thank you for your letter.  If I can be of further
assistance, please do not hesitate to call at 916/322-6441.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Alfredo Flores
                                        Research Specialist
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division


AF:gb


Melvin Shapiro
3701 7th Ave. #6
San Diego, CA 92103

FPPC

NOV 15 11 30 AM '76

November 12, 1976

Fair Political Practices Comm.
1100 K St
Sacramento, Ca 95814

Dear Sir,

I would appreciate answers to the following questions:

1) Were City Councilmen required to file income disclosure statements under section
87207 in 1976? If they were, why didn't San Diego Councilmen file?

2)Are planning commissioners "designated employees" for filing purposes under
Conflict of Interest Codes?

3)What is the deadline for local Conflict of Interest Codes?

4)What was the Commission's opinion on #75-119

Sincerely,

Melvin Shapiro

STOA, All candidates
